qa_pairs.q:
Good morning!
We're delighted to spend these 3 nights in your apartment!
We're flying from Madrid and on Friday we land in Lisbon around 19:15, so between getting out, picking up the rental car and arriving there I calculate we can arrive around 21:00.
On the other hand, on Monday the flight leaves late (20:00), so I'm asking if it's possible to leave the house later or at least be able to leave our luggage in the house until at least 17:00.
We're traveling with two girls aged 7 and 2, the older one will sleep on the sofa but for the little one we would need a crib.
If I have any questions I'll contact you!
Greetings and thanks in advance.
qa_pairs.a:
Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination on your GPS.  Some maps have the house address slightly off.  The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right. The entrance is a white door with the number 5 above and a digital pad to the right.

The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian but you are allowed to drive by and drop off your bags and passengers. Just be aware that the road is narrow and it gets narrower further ahead of the house. If you want to avoid the most narrow part of the road, make sure to turn left on the first street after the house, and then left again. 

The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona (https://maps.app.goo.gl/d6AUVx1ph5L7LAja6) and the fastest way is to go straight ahead after the house (through the narrow road) and follow it as it bends to the right.  It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 27 euros per day and it's an 8 minute walk.

There is also parking on the streets (starting at 170 meters from the house) around the Church de Nossa Senhora dos Navegantes but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. 

You will also find free parking 12 minutes away walking. We recommend you put "Cascais Beauty Clinic" on your map. That road (Rua Dom José de Avilez) is the closest street with free street parking. If you don't find anything on that street, you can put "Citadel Park Condominium" from there and on your way, there you will see several streets with free parking, including  Rua Franklin Lamas (which at most puts 15 minutes away walking).

We have this information with maps on our website:
https://casarosahouse.com/parking/

Here are self check-in instructions:

Look for the white door on the side of Beco Dos Invalidos alley. There is a number 5 above the door and a keypad to the right. Please enter the code 787870 and then Press E on the keypad.

Then look for the door to the right (door #1). Enter the same code on this door and then press the lower bigger button after the code.

See the video below:
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/cpei6nu38seqsui465js9/SelfCheckIn_1.MOV?rlkey=7gmu8o8yy4mbax5pb9aroi3vx&dl=0

The sofa bed and the and the crib are setup. 

If you run into any issues entering please call us at +351-912-947-429.

We will also need to collect 24 euros of tourist tax (it's 4 euros per adult per night). You can pay it in cash or I can send you a request so you pay it via Airbnb. Please let me know which you prefer. We are leaving you Cascais museum tourist passes which entitle you to enter a number of museums for free for 24 hours. They are issued by the city for your tourist tax payment.

qa_pairs.q:
You can send me the request so I can pay it via Airbnb. 
Thank you!
qa_pairs.a:
Here it is:

https://www.airbnb.com/resolutions/CLA-9JNQPPYCK3

Please let us know if you need anything or have any questions.

Also, we get great feedback from our guests for recommending Euphoria cafe, very close by:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/TcimwigVipquXZMG6

They have the best coffee in town and a very good set of breakfast choices. 

And also for the restaurant "Baía Do Peixe". They have excellent seafood with delicious octopus and a great view to the bay (try to reserve a table upstairs with a view). You need to reserve in advance.

Enjoy

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] , 

Your apartment is ready. 

Here's the link for the tourist tax payment:
https://www.airbnb.com/resolutions/CLA-FEPKCKJ8J5

And here's you self check-in information:

Your code is 787880. Press E after the code on the main building door. This same code will get you thru all three doors. 

Next door is in front of the main door (has #0 on it). Press the code and then the bigger button at the button. It's always good to pull the door from the handle when opening and push when closing to prevent jamming from the electric bolt. This door doesn't have autolock, but a magnet, so if the code doesn't do anything it's because it is open already, you just need to push it. 

Your apartment is one floor up (door #2). The one with a cat and a flower sticker next to the door. Use the same code. There are motion sensors to turn the lights on everywhere, if it is dark and the lights don't come on, just wave and it should come on. This video explains the entrance to your apartment.

 https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/ri3ddiguho0nvpv2wvf6d/SelfCheckIn_2.MOV?rlkey=feek3urugwcrnehii94kbomnz&dl=0 

To exit you don't need to enter the code when exiting, just press the lower bigger button. If you run into any issues entering please call me at +351-912-947-429. 

We are leaving you museum tourist passes on the table. They are issued by the city of Cascais for your tourist tax payment. On the back of the tickets you will see a list of museums that you can access for free for a period of 24 hours. Let me know if you wish some museum recommendations.

For traditional portuguese chicken, "Somos un regalo" is extremely popular with locals. There is often a line at 6:45 PM waiting for them to open at 7 PM. qa_pairs.a:
Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination on your GPS.  Some maps have the house address slightly off.  The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right. The entrance is a white door with the number 5 above and a digital pad to the right.

The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian but you are allowed to drive by and drop off your bags and passengers. Just be aware that the road is narrow and it gets narrower further ahead of the house. If you want to avoid the most narrow part of the road, make sure to turn left on the first street after the house, and then left again. 

The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona (https://maps.app.goo.gl/d6AUVx1ph5L7LAja6) and the fastest way is to go straight ahead after the house (through the narrow road) and follow it as it bends to the right.  It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 20 euros per day and it's an 8 minute walk.

There is also parking on the streets (starting at 170 meters from the house) around the Church de Nossa Senhora dos Navegantes but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. 

You will also find free parking 12 minutes away walking. We recommend you put "Cascais Beauty Clinic" on your map. That road (Rua Dom José de Avilez) is the closest street with free street parking. If you don't find anything on that street, you can put "Citadel Park Condominium" from there and on your way, there you will see several streets with free parking, including  Rua Franklin Lamas (which at most puts 15 minutes away walking).

[GUEST] send you check-in instructions next.

Please enter the code 786530 and then Press E. Your apartment is #1 (the door on the right). Use the same code and press the big button under.

qa_pairs.a:
Unfortunately it doesn't look like the apartment will be ready much earlier than 3 PM. But you are welcome to drop your bags, just please message us here about 10/15 mins before you arrive so our helper can be expecting for you.

We will send you detailed instructions with a unique code for you to enter (there are no keys) shortly. Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination when calling Uber or Bolt (cheaper than Uber). Drivers often miss the house when using the address. The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian and cars are allowed to dropoff and pickup passengers. The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right.

Here are your self check-in instructions:

The house is directly across the street from the front entrance to the restaurant "Armazem 22". Look for the white door with #5 on top and a keypad to the right.

Your code is 786660. This code will get you thru all three doors. Press E after the code on the main door.

Next door is in front of the main door (has #0 on it). Press the code and then the bigger button at the button. It's always good to pull the door from the handle when opening and push when closing to prevent jamming from the electric bolt. This door doesn't have autolock, but a magnet, so if the code doesn't do anything it's because it is open already, you just need to push it.

Your apartment is one floor up (door #2). The one with a cat and a flower sticker next to the door. Use the same code.

There are motion sensors to turn the lights on everywhere, if it is dark and the lights don't come on, just wave and it should come on.

This video explains the entrance to your apartment.
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/ri3ddiguho0nvpv2wvf6d/SelfCheckIn_2.MOV?rlkey=feek3urugwcrnehii94kbomnz&dl=0

To exit you don't need to enter the code when exiting, just press the lower bigger button.

If you run into any issues entering please call us at +351-912-947-429.

qa_pairs.a:
Thanks for the info on the certificate. We'll check it.

The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona (https://maps.app.goo.gl/d6AUVx1ph5L7LAja6) and the fastest way is to go straight ahead after the house (through the narrow road) and follow it as it bends to the right.  It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 20 euros per day and it's an 8 minute walk.

There is also parking on the streets (starting at 170 meters from the house) around the Church de Nossa Senhora dos Navegantes but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. 

You will also find free parking 12 minutes away walking. We recommend you put "Cascais Beauty Clinic" on your map. That road (Rua Dom José de Avilez) is the closest street with free street parking. If you don't find anything on that street, you can put "Citadel Park Condominium" from there and on your way, there you will see several streets with free parking, including  Rua Franklin Lamas (which at most puts 15 minutes away walking).

We also have a website:
https://casarosahouse.com

Parking info on the website:
https://casarosahouse.com/parking/

Hi again Claudia. I just noticed that your bus arrives to Oriente station in Lisbon. The public transportation option from there is to take the subway (with a connection) to Cais de Sodre and then take the train to Cascais, and then walk for 10 minutes to the house. It should be about 90 minutes if everything is running on time and will run about 5 euros each. 

Uber is not very expensive here, so you may wish to consider that. Bolt is a bit cheaper than Uber, and should be between 20 and 25 euros and take about 30-35 minutes. 

If you happen to arrive early, no worries, you can leave your bags and go enjoy the town. Just let us know if you do come earlier so we can tell our helper to be on the lookout for you. There is one next door to your apartment. The next apartment to your left (door #0) has the same type of lock as yours. You can enter code 9317 there. As soon as you enter on your immediate right you will see a washer and a dryer behind a little courtain that you can pull up. You are free to use it as needed. Let me know if you have any questions. Also,  we just need to collect the Cascais Municipal Tourist Tax of 4 euros per adult per night, for  a total of 36 euros. Please let us know if you wish to pay it in cash or if you would like to pay it over Airbnb.

Also, we get great feedback from our guests for recommending Euphoria cafe, very close by:

Enjoy We can pay cash .

qa_pairs.a:
The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona (https://maps.app.goo.gl/d6AUVx1ph5L7LAja6) and the fastest way is to go straight ahead after the house (through the narrow road) and follow it as it bends to the right.  It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 20 euros per day and it's an 8 minute walk.

There is also parking on the streets (starting at 170 meters from the house) around the Church de Nossa Senhora dos Navegantes but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. 

You will also find free parking 12 minutes away walking. We recommend you put "Cascais Beauty Clinic" on your map. That road (Rua Dom José de Avilez) is the closest street with free street parking. If you don't find anything on that street, you can put "Citadel Park Condominium" from there and on your way, there you will see several streets with free parking, including  Rua Franklin Lamas (which at most puts 15 minutes away walking).

We will have the crib setup for you.

Everything is steps away, and we will message you as soon as the apartment is ready. Just let us know 10 minutes before you arrive so our helper can be expecting you.
 
Also there is a Cascais city tourist tax of 4 euros per adult per night for the first seven nights that we have to collect from you. You can pay cash or you can send it via Airbnb. Please let us know which you prefer.

qa_pairs.a:
If you are near a train station on the line that starts at Cais de Sodre, that's a nice way. Otherwise Uber or Bolt (usually a bit cheaper than Uber) are good ride sharing options. Either way, please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination. Drivers often miss the house when using the address. The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian and cars are allowed to dropoff and pickup passengers. The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right. If you walk from the train station, you can use that reference as well, just make sure you go to the front entrance to the restaurant, not the back (which you might pass by when walking).

Hi. From the airport there is a subway that connects to the train to Cascais. But I'm Portugal Uber and Bolt are very cheap, so for 20-25 euros you can arrive to the house from the airport. To the center of Lisbon there's a very easy train that takes 45 minutes. The traffic station in Cascais is 10 minutes from the house. We will send you detailed instructions with a code to enter the apartment tomorrow morning.

The public transportation option from the airport is to take the subway (with a connection) to Cais de Sodre and then take the train to Cascais, and then walk for 10 minutes to the house. It should be about 90 minutes if everything is running on time and will run about 5 euros each. 

The ride-hailing option we recommend is Bolt which is a bit cheaper than Uber, and should be between 20 and 25 euros and take about 30-35 minutes. 

Have a good flight! Ricardo

At the front door keypad enter code 786270. Press E after the code on the main door. This is your code for all 3 doors.

Next door is in front of the main door. Press the code and then the bigger button at the button. It's always good to pull the door from the handle when opening and push when closing to prevent jamming from the electric bolt. This door doesn't have autolock, but a magnet, so if the code doesn't do anything it's because it is open already, you just need to push it.

Go one floor up the stairs. Your apartment is there, the one with a cat and a flower sticker next to the door. Use the same code.

There are motion sensors to turn the lights on everywhere, if it is dark and the lights don't come on, just wave and it should come on.

Once inside the building, your door is in front of the main door. Enter the same code and then the big button under it.

Then go to the top floor. You will have another lock. Press the same code and the big button under it.

qa_pairs.a:
We will send you detailed instructions tomorrow with a unique code for you to enter (there are no keys). We have a checkout tomorrow, so the apartment might not be ready until closer to 3 PM but you are welcome to drop bags off starting at 11 AM when our helper arrives. 

Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination on your GPS.  Some maps have the house address slightly off.  The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right. The entrance is a white door with the number 5 above and a digital pad to the right.

The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian but you are allowed to drive by and drop off your bags and passengers. If you message us 10 minutes before you arrive, we can ask our helper to come out so that she can show you were to drop your bags off. You might get a car or two behind you, but drivers are usually very patient on these semi-pedestrian roads, so don't stress, take  your time.

Also, be aware that the road is narrow and it gets narrower further ahead of the house. If you want to avoid the most narrow part of the road, make sure to turn left on the first street after the house, and then left again. 

The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona (https://maps.app.goo.gl/d6AUVx1ph5L7LAja6) and the fastest way is to go straight ahead after the house (through the narrow road) and follow it as it bends to the right.  It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 28 euros per day.

There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. 

[GUEST] and Ricardo

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] . Thanks for letting us know. I think it is fixed now:
https://casarosahouse.com/getting-to-from-sintra-lisbon-and-porto-without-a-car/

But the short answer is, there is no bus service. The only public transportation option is the subway and then walking and then the train, and then walk some more. Uber and especially Bolt are very cheap in Portugal. A trip for two people from the airport will cost you about 20 euros outside rush hour. That's just a bit more than the train and subway tickets.

Yes, no worries, you can arrive whenever you wish.  Here are your self check-in instructions:

You have one passcode for all the doors. Your code is: 768765 

Press E after the code on the main door.

Next door is right in front of the main door. Press the code and then the bigger button at the button. It's always good to pull the door from the handle when opening and push when closing to prevent jamming from the electric bolt. This door doesn't have autolock, but a magnet, so if the code doesn't do anything it's because it is open already, you just need to push it.

Go one floor up the stairs. Your apartment is there, the one with a cat and a flower sticker next to the door. Use the same code.

There are motion sensors to turn the lights on everywhere, if it is dark and the lights don't come on, just wave and it should come on.

This video explains the entrance to your apartment.
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/ri3ddiguho0nvpv2wvf6d/SelfCheckIn_2.MOV?rlkey=feek3urugwcrnehii94kbomnz&dl=0

To exit you don't need to enter the code when exiting, just press the lower bigger button.

If you run into problems entering please call us at +351-912-947-429.

Also, please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination. Drivers often miss the house when driving. And we recommend Uber or Bolt (often cheaper than Uber in Portugal) over taxis from the airport, as taxis often charge much higher prices.

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] . Thank you. The apartment will be ready by 3 PM. We will have the sofa bed setup and 3 beds in the bedroom. 

Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination on your GPS.  Some maps have the house address slightly off.  The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right. The entrance is a white door with the number 5 above and a digital pad to the right.

The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian but you are allowed to drive by and drop off your bags and passangers. Just be aware that the road is narrow and it gets narrower further ahead of the house. If you want to avoid the most narrow part of the road, make sure to turn left on the first street after the house, and then left again. 

The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona (https://maps.app.goo.gl/d6AUVx1ph5L7LAja6) and the fastest way is to go straight ahead after the house (through the narrow road) and follow it as it bends to the right.  It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 28 euros per day.

There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. 

There is also free parking available about a 15 to 20 minute walk from the house. If you drive to "Luna House cowork", that street has free parking.

Sending you instructions for entering the apartment in a separate message in a few minutes.

Please let us know if it as all possible to store our luggage or to possibly check in early.  Or if not, if you have any other suggestions for places to wait with the luggage. We also have a question about the required government form.  We are wondering if it is possible to present that information to you in person upon arrival rather than sending over the internet? Please let us know if that is not possible.
Thank you!
[GUEST] and [GUEST] Roberts Hi [GUEST] . Yes, you are welcome to come drop your bags off starting at 11 AM when our helper arrives to start cleaning. Unfortunately we will not be able to meet you when you arrive. And nowadays, all information is transmitted eletronically to Portuguese immigrations, so if you could complete that form we would appreciate it. Our system only passes it through and doesn't share it. It is a requirement for us, just like hotels, and the fines are 500 euros for each infraction if fail to collect and get caught. As for early check-in, we can't promise it. We have a checkout that day. But if we do manage to finish cleaning earlier, we will let you know. Everything is steps away from the house. Thank you. We also will complete the form and return back as per your required process--We thought it worth at least asking if it might have been possible to do in person instead as we have sometimes had that option in the past in hotels and some other Airbnbs and try to minimize the flow of our personal information over the web when possible.
Thanks again. We are looking forward to our stay.
[GUEST] and [GUEST]

qa_pairs.q: Hello- I am visiting in a couple months. Do you have a recommendation on the best way to get to Sintra from the Airbnb?
qa_pairs.a:
Thank you for your reservation. 

The best is to take Uber or Bolt (the cheaper competitor). Whatever you do, don't drive there. It's very hard to find parking and the roads are hard to navigate.

Also, we are working on a website with tips including transportation from the airport:
http://casarosahouse.com/

It's not 100% finished, but feel free to check it out. If you have any questions prior to your arrival please let us know!

Best,
[GUEST] & Ricardo

qa_pairs.q:
Thank you! I actually largely ask because Sintra seems to be the best way to get a bus/train to Porto. Porto is our next stop once we leave Caiscais so if you have a better recommendation on how to get to Porto, please let me know. Sintra is worth visiting. But never heard of anyne going there to go to Porto. There is a direct bus from Cascais to Porto (Flixbus). The stop in Cascais is not in the center, so you still need a 10 minute taxi ride there. Another frequent option is to take the fast trains from Lisbon (Oriente station). To get to Oriente you probably want to take a Bolt (about 22 euros). Hi [GUEST] and Ricardo - Are there any catamaran excursions (snorkeling or sunset cruise) in Caiscias that you recommend? Hi [GUEST] . Over a year ago we rented a boat and have us a nice tour of the river for 8 of us. I can't remember how we found them, but kept their number. The name was Ekaterina and the phone number: +351-93-993-2626. We really enjoyed it and would recommend them. They had several packages, perhaps one of them will suit you. Also, we've heard good things about https://www.cascaisdive.com/

I know they do scuba diving, not sure about other activities. Form completed. Thank you.

Obrigado that will be helpful as we will go to Sinatra and do not want to leaves items in car. we recommend going to Quinta de Regaleira in Sintra instead of Pena  Palace which gets very crowded this time of the year. Regardless, if you are driving, you will want to go early to find parking near the center. If you do go to Pena Palace, cars are not allowed to go up the mountain (where it is located), so you would need to park near the center and then walk up or take a car or tuktuk. We would suggest you avoid those Tuktuks, they are very expensive and not worth it. Instead, if you don't want to walk up, it's better to take Uber or Bolt. Quinta de Regaleira is a lot easier to walk from the center.

qa_pairs.q: Hi, could you please advise where we can park if we rent a car? Since we have a one year old boy, do you know if Bolt has car seats for baby typically? Or do you recommend to rent a car? We are planning to visit sintra during our stay:) Hi [GUEST] . Unfortunately I don't think we have a great answer for this. Bolt does provide booster seats for 5-12 year olds, but it doesn't have anything for 1 year olds. And I don't think Uber is any better. We generally don't recommend renting a car unless you are going to be going to several places far away from Cascais. It's just not worth the hassle. Sintra is very close by, and it isn't easy at all to find parking there, so even if you had rented a car, we would advice you to still take Bolt were it not for the fact that you have a baby. Perhaps the best option might be to fly with the car seat. I know in the US all airlines have to accept them for free (they usually check them in at the gate). The rules are for international flights are technically up to the airline, but I would be very surprised if they would charge you for it. You could call the airline to double check. Hi [GUEST] . Please complete the form and your arrival information as soon as possible. Thank you We completed the form Can we request an early check in? You are welcome to drop bags at starting at 11 am though. Thank you [GUEST] . One quick question, is there a blender in the apartment? Can we request one if not? Thank you so much. qa_pairs.a:
We will send you detailed instructions tomorrow with a unique code for you to enter (there are no keys). You are welcome to drop bags off starting at 11 AM, and we will let you know if the apartment is ready before 3 PM.

Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination on your GPS.  Some maps have the house address slightly off.  The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right. The entrance is a white door with the number 5 above and a digital pad to the right.

The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian but you are allowed to drive by and drop off your bags and passangers. Just be aware that the road is narrow and it gets narrower further ahead of the house. If you want to avoid the most narrow part of the road, make sure to turn left on the first street after the house, and then left again. 

The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona (https://maps.app.goo.gl/d6AUVx1ph5L7LAja6) and the fastest way is to go straight ahead after the house (through the narrow road) and follow it as it bends to the right.  It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 28 euros per day.

There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. 

There is also free parking available about a 15 to 20 minute walk from the house. If you drive to "Luna House cowork", that street has free parking.

[GUEST] and Ricardo

I also read good reviews for Restaurante Baía, Aqafarina, Gusto Pizzeria, and Chutnify. Any thoughts on those? Thanks, [GUEST] Hi [GUEST] . We do recommend reservations. The high season is not over. Of the ones you mentioned, we really liked chutnify, it's a bit pricey, but they have good food and an amazing drink which unfortunately can't remember the name (it's reddish and alcoholic of it helps). We have mixed reviews on house of wonders and Masala is still out overall noon seafood favorite. Any place you'd recommend for fish (not shellfish or cephalopods)? Like sea bass, filet of sole, etc. Both  "Dom [GUEST] I" and "Baia Do Peixe" have good fresh fish options. Dom [GUEST] I is a small family restaurant open only weekdays. "Baia Do Peixe" has a great view to the bay (try to reserve a table upstairs with a view).

Enjoy Dom [GUEST] I has unfortunately closed. We saw them open a few days ago. Perhaps they've gone on holidays but permanent closing would be surprising

qa_pairs.q: We are getting excited about our trip and working out final plans! We may have a few questions in the coming weeks.  One question, do you provide laundry detergent for your washer? A second question,  does Bolt work well for a group of 5? We will need transportation to Sintra one day and then to Obidos for our next accommodation when we check out of your apartment. Is Bolt the best option? Obrigado! Hi [GUEST] . We do provide detergent, and Bolt does have an option for extra passangers (XL or Van). They are not as readily available as the regular Bolts, so the wait is often longer, but you should be fine. You can also have Uber as a backup (they have the same XL service for up to 6 pax). And to be clear, yes, Bolt is absolutely the best option to get to Sintra. It's a not easy to drive there. Obidos is further and easier to drive and park. But if that's the only reason you would consider renting a car, then not worth it, Bolt is your best bet. For the rest of our travels we will use public transportation, those were the only two trips where public transportation did not seem feasible. Thank you for confirming that Bolt makes more sense than renting a car! Hi [GUEST] . Please complete that form as soon as you can. Thank you. I have just submitted the form!

The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is 28 euros per day.

There is also free parking available about a 15 to 20 minute walk from the house. If you drive to "Luna House cowork", that street has free parking.

You can stop in front of the house to unload though. It is a semi-pedestrian road, but you are allowed to go through. There are some narrow parts though. 

It is easiest to put "Armazem 22" on your GPS and stop there. The restaurant will be on your left and the house on the right.

We will send you full instructions to get in tomorrow if we aren't able to come meet you.

qa_pairs.a:
You have several parking lots nearby. There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time.

Hi [GUEST] . Thanks for the registration.

We will send you detailed instructions tomorrow with a unique code for you to enter (there are no keys). And since you are arriving late, we will give you our home number that you can call 24/7 in case of trouble getting in.

Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination when calling Uber or Bolt (cheaper than Uber). We don't recommend taxis as they tend to charge whatever they feel like to Cascais. Bolt should be about 25 euros, Uber 30 euros. Drivers often miss the house when using the address. The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian and cars are allowed to dropoff and pickup passengers. The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right.

I see that you wrote that you wanted to have a car. Honestly, it's a lot easier not to rent a car at the airport,  especially after a long flight, their waits are long. There is at least one rental car company walking distance in Cascais (Hertz). You may take a look there. Let us know if you need further recommendations. Going to Sintra and Lisbon with an Uber will be far cheaper and less stressful than a car.

[GUEST] and Ricardo

You have several parking lots nearby. There is also parking on the streets behind the church but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. Sorry, pressed the send button before I could finish... Ignore the previous message, here it is corrected:

Hi [GUEST] , about your question about restaurant recommendations in Cascais. We get great feedback from our guests for recommending Baia Do Peixe. It has a really delicious octopus (also a very generous portion) and great seafood in general. You want to reserve in advance. If you can get a table upstairs with a view of the bay, even better. We have heard from good friends good reviews from Cantinho deo Avillez. The Chef is internationally recognized and has several other restaurants. 

For coffee and breakfast we recommend Euphoria cafe, very close by. They have the best coffee in town and a very good set of breakfast choices. 

Our website casarosahouse.com has several other restaurant recommendations.

Enjoy and let us know if you have any other questions please

qa_pairs.a:
Good morning [GUEST] . Your apartment is ready. Your code is 792765.

This code gets you into the building, into the apartment and to get your bags.

On the main door Press the letter E after the numbers.

One inside, to access your bags you need to enter thru the door directly in front of the main door.

Your apartment door will be on the right. Use the same code and press the big button under.

See the video below:
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/cpei6nu38seqsui465js9/SelfCheckIn_1.MOV?rlkey=7gmu8o8yy4mbax5pb9aroi3vx&dl=0

If you run into any issues entering please call us at +351-912-947-429.

Please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination on your GPS.  Some maps have the house address slightly off.  The restaurant will be to your left and the pink house to your right. The entrance is a white door with the number 5 above and a digital pad to the right.

The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian but you are allowed to drive by and drop off your bags and passengers. Just be aware that the road is narrow and it gets narrower further ahead of the house. If you want to avoid the most narrow part of the road, make sure to turn left on the first street after the house, and then left again. 

The easiest parking location is at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona but we heard from a previous guest that it might be closed because they are using it as a staging for the Iron Man. 

There is also parking on the streets (starting at 170 meters from the house around the Church de Nossa Senhora dos Navegantes but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. So that's only convenient for a short while.

On a weekend like today, your best bet might be to park on the streets with free parking, which are about 12 minutes away walking. We recommend you put "Cascais Beauty Clinic" on your map. That road (Rua Dom José de Avilez) is the closest street with free street parking. If you don't find anything on that street, you can put "Citadel Park Condominium" from there and on your way, there you will see several streets with free parking, including  Rua Franklin Lamas (which at most puts 15 minutes away walking).

Hope that helps!

qa_pairs.q: Hi there, is there parking available nearby?
qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] , 

Thank you for your reservation. You have several parking lots nearby. There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is about 14 euros per day now during low season.

There is also free parking available about a 15 to 20 minute walk from the house. If you drive to "Luna House cowork", that street has free parking.

qa_pairs.a:
Thanks for the info. Here is some info on how to get to the apartment from the airport. The public transportation option is taking the subway from the airport to Cais de Sodre, which is the train station in Lisbon with trains to Cascais. The total travel time would be about 90 minutes, plus a 10 minute walk in Cascais to get to the apartment. 

Alternatively, Uber and Bolt are fairly cheap in Portugal. You should expect to pay about 25 euros with Bolt or about 30 with Uber from the airport during non-rush hour and it would take about 35-40 minutes. 

The apartment is located on a semi-pedestrian road, but cars are allowed to go thru and drop off passangers. If you take Uber/Bolt or a taxi, please use the restaurant "Armazem 22" as the destination. Sometimes drivers miss the house by the address. If you walk from the train station also put that restaurant as the destination on your GPS.

I will do my best to meet you tomorrow, but otherwise will be sending you self check-in instructions. The apartment should be ready by 2 PM, if you arrive earlier, you are welcome to drop bags off.

But Oriente is closer to the highway and the trip from there will probably be shorter for you. Santa Apolonia station has exits on 2 sides. I recommend arriving a corner before requesting transport to avoid confusion. There are also plenty of taxis outside the station, but prices are negotiable to Cascais and will certainly be much more expensive than Uber or Bolt.

qa_pairs.q: We just looked at your website. It is great!  It has given us a lot of information that we were needing. We are so excited. We would also be interested in any information you may have on the other towns and interests Lisbon of course. Then Sintra is very close by and a must see. Obidos is further and requires a car but it can be very nice if some festival is happening there. South there is a beach town of Sisimbra and next to it an idealic desolate beach called Praia do Ribeiro do Cavalo. We love it. You can get to it by hiking downhill a bit of an adventourous path (perhaps dangerous depending on your comfort), but the view as you go down is very very nice. Or a water taxi from Sisimbra (which runs during the summer, not sure if it will be running in April). Is a small scooter acceptable to get to these locations? Other than Cascais, not really. Cascais is the only flat place with even pavement. Buses or other means? For Lisbon you have train, buses and very cheap Uber. For Sintra, also cheap uber (about 15 euros each way) and also buses (a bit more complicated). You could probably get to Lisbon to the other places and then take Uber from there. Uber is cheap in Portugal, about 20 euros for a half hour ride. So even to go to Obidos or Sisimbra, you are not looking at more than 70 euros each way. Unless you are planning on going to many more places far away, renting a car would wind up being more expensive and far more troublesome. Also, Bolt is a cheaper alternative to Uber in Portugal. Do you have parking available
qa_pairs.a:
We don't have our own spot. The historic center is small and almost no one does. There is parking on the streets and in April it should be easily available, but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. Not convienient for days at a time. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is about 14 euros per day.

There is also free parking in the streets outside of the center, about a 15 to 20 minute walk.

Both are in the center of Cascais. However, there is a subway station at the airport, and you can take it to Cais do Sodre (changing subways once). From Cais do Sodre you can take the train to Cascais. In total your trip would be between 1.5 to 2 hours. How has the weather been? It's been rainy for the past few days unfortunately. Better for the next week I hope We changed plans. qa_pairs.a:
One of us will be at the house to meet you. I see you said you will arrive between 2 and 3 PM. We will try to have the apartment ready by the time you arrive. But at the very least you can drop bags off. If you would like you can drive by the house and drop bags off before you park. The road is a narrow semi-pedestrian road, but cars are allowed to drop bags off. 

As for parking, you have several paid parking lots nearby. There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. The price is about 14 euros per day.

There is also free parking available about a 15 to 20 minute walk from the house. If you drive to "Luna House cowork", that street has free parking.

Just message us tomorrow when you have a more precise estimated arrival time please so we can be on the lookout to meet you by the door.

qa_pairs.a:
We have several parking options in walking distance from the house. 
There is parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It’s a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don’t have to worry about adding time. I think the price is 28 euros per day.

There is also free parking available about a 15 to 20 minute walk from the house. If you drive to “Luna House cowork”, that street has free parking.

I suggest you put the restaurant "Armazem 22" as your destination. The restaurant will be on the left and the house on the right. It's a narrow semi pedestrian road, you will be able to stop for a minute to unload and then go park. Alternatively, if you don't have much luggage,  you can park and walk from there.

You have several parking lots nearby. There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time.

Taxis are also available, but their prices vary a lot and I have heard of outrageously high prices from some guests. Put the destination of "Armazem 22" on the app. They sometimes miss it by the address. If you have any problems getting in, please call me directly at +351-912-947-429.

Also, the pickup lot at the airport is called "kiss and fly", located next to the departures area, on the floor above from where you exit when you arrive.

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] , we are looking forward to hosting you. You have several parking lots nearby. There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time. I think the price is 28 euros per day.

[GUEST] Hi [GUEST] , sorry for the delay replying. There are a number of museums in Cascais within minutes walk from the apartment. I suggest you look into what interests you there. In Lisbon you have an outstanding oceanarium: https://www.oceanario.pt/en.
You also have a great deal of museums in Lisbon, the science museum being one of our favorites (next to oceanarium).

The type of rain you will likely experience is not going to be too bad. You may enjoy going to Sintra (we recommend Quinta de Regaleira) even with a bit of rain. Just bring an umbrella.  And of course there are a number of excellent restaurants in the area. We have recommendations on our website.

You probably want to go to the beach as soon as you arrive tomorrow as it may be the warmest during your stay and looks like no rain the whole day. We enjoy Cascais very much with mild rainy days, you just want to monitor for pockets with no rain or little rain and avoid heavy rain.

The tourist buses that run around Lisbon, Belem and Cascais are all covered, so that is also something you can leave for a mild rainy day. That's great Ricardo, thanks very much.

The road by the house is narrow semi-pedestrian but normal size cars have no issue going thru and are allowed to dropoff and pickup passengers.

Our helper Anastasia will be waiting for you to check you in. Please message us here when you're in the car at the airport and also 5 minutes before you arrive to make sure she's on the lookout for you.

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] . Please find instructions below:

If you are taking an Uber or Bolt from the Lisbon airport keep in mind that the pickup area is in a parking lot upstairs, just outside of the Departures section/floor, not the Arrivals floor which is what you would see in many other airports. Make sure to tell the taxi or Uber/Bolt driver to take you to the restaurant Armazem 22 in the center of Cascais. We have found that they often miss it when given the address. 

The restaurant is right across from the house. The restaurant will be on your left when coming the narrow stone road, and the house on the right. 

Your code is 282882. You will need this code for all 3 doors. For the main building door, you enter this code followed by the E button on the lower right. Then you have 3 seconds to push the door. There are sensors to turn the lights on in the hallway, if they don't, just wave. 

Now you want to go through the door directly in front. Here you enter the code followed by the bigger button at the bottom. Once inside, you will then see stairs, you need to go up all 3 flights of stairs to the top apartment. There are light sensors everywhere. Once you reach the top, you then have another lock identical to the one below. Use the same code here. By the way, on these locks, when you exit, you don't need to enter the code, just the bottom larger button.

Here is a video:
https://youtu.be/4mTo524l428

If you run into any problems entering, please call us at +351-21-580-6103. That's our land line, the only phone we will hear late at night. 

Once again, your code is:
282882

qa_pairs.a:
Glad all is going well!

You have the advantage of being light travelers, so I think checking out Lisbon is a good plan. The train ride to Lisbon is a very nice ride overlooking the water at many spots (seat on the right side). The only issue with it is that there are ocassional strikes this month. But hopefully that won't be the case for you.

If you have time, I would suggest making a stop at Belem with the train and walk around there. The stop is very close to everything there. You can see the Jeronimo monastery, check out Pasteis de Belem (the best known bakery in Portugal) and the tower of Belem... 

The last stop on the train is Cais Do Sodre in the center of Lisbon. You have a lot to see very close to that stop. If you are still have time and want to take a break from walking, then you can jump on the bus, which leaves from Praça da Figueira, all closeby... You could even go to the airport by Subway if you manage to jump off the bus near a subway stop (the airport is on the red line). Alternatively if you want to continue walking, you can head to the Alfama or Graca neighborhoods which are very unique and not that far.

http://www.casarosahouse.com

One tip on our website that guests often find useful about heading to Porto from Cascais is that there is a direct bus. The train is of course nicer, but you have to change trains in Lisbon and they are at different stations so that makes it complicated and time consuming:

https://shop.flixbus.dk/search?departureCity=357d27b9-4d7c-46f3-84bf-7bdcf1bbba5f&arrivalCity=74923e7b-3ace-43d2-8278-41ee00193a85&route=Cascais-Porto

Let us know if you have any questions or we can help in any way plan your trip.

Best,

[GUEST]

You can message here. I recommend that you come to the house to unload and then go park.

For parking you have several parking lots nearby. There is also parking on the streets but you have to pay with coins and you may get lucky and park close to the house or not. The easiest is to park at Estacionamento Marechal Carmona which is 500 meters away. It's a very large parking lot so there is always parking there and you pay when you exit so you don't have to worry about adding time.

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST]. As promised, here is an idea for a plan on your way to Porto:

Stop in Belem, try to eat at Pasteis de Belem (the best known bakery in Portugal).
Check out the tower of Belem, walk outside the Jeromino monastery 

Then go to Lisbon, park in the Mirador de Graca (viewing point of Lisbon). From there you can walk downhill towards Alfama. Then you can get back to your car by taxi if you don't want to walk up.

On your way to Porto you can stop in Coimbra, the center is small but nice, you an then go the University which has beautiful views and there is a beautiful university library: https://visit.uc.pt/en/space-list/joanina

Closer to Porto, there is a town called Aveiro, it is also called the Venice of Portugal. I think that's an exageration, but spending an hour there is nice and on the way.

Hope you are enjoying Cascais and the apartment. Let us know if you need anything!

Good afternoon [GUEST] . Hope you are enjoying Cascais and the apartment. Please let us know if you need anything or have any questions. 

[GUEST] wanted me to give you some recommendations on must see places. My top suggestions would be:
- Alfama and Graça in Lisbon. 
- Sintra (the city center, Pena Castle and Quinta Da Regaleira)

To get to Lisbon you have the train which takes you to the center. From there you can easily walk to Alfama and around the center.  I would put a Miradouro Graça and walk that way from the train station, and walk back around a slightly different way. That should give you a nice walking tour.

qa_pairs.q: If you are planning on going to Sintra, I recommend both Pena Castle and Quinta da Regaleira. Getting there by car can be a bit tricky, they don't let you park near the castles nowadays and the roads are one way, so if you need to turn around from the castle to the center, it's a 25 minute drive back. It's usually easiest to park in the center, and not try to go up all the way to the castles with the car, instead take Bolt or Uber from there. Many thanks Ricardo! Very useful.  [GUEST] and [GUEST]

Hi [GUEST] . Congratulations on the international school prospect.

For longer term rentals, idealist.pt is the most common website where realtors list properties, and sometimes owners too.  But there is also https://www.olx.pt/d/imoveis/q-alugar/ which is used by regular people and you may find shorter term rentals there (though probably not just one month). There is also Facebook marketplace which is used quite a bit in Portugal. 

We also know a very good realtor, but she only does longer term rentals (1+ year). Her name is [GUEST] , and her number is +351 917 762 080. She specializes in the Cascais area. I spoke to her recently and she told me that right now availability is very low. But I would suggest you keep her number for the future.

If you wish to stay in our apartment, where you are now, it is available 4 more days after your current checkout (until the 12th). We would charge the same price/night you already paid and give you a cash discount. That would be 50 euros/night.

We also have another apartment (right on top of you) which is currently available from the 7th of November to the 22nd, but it is much bigger, 3 bedroom and remodeled, so it is quite a bit more expensive. Right now it is low season though, so, you might get good deals on Airbnb, especially since you are only looking till mid December. The prices do go higher around Christmas and New Years.

If you wish to extend your stay for more days, do let me know so I can block the calendar.

qa_pairs.q:
Hello Ricardo I would be happy to host you.  This is a very standard room but has everything you need and is at an excellent location. - Please note that the exact address is:

515 W 111th St Apt 2C (not W 110th St)

The building main entrance says “513-515.”  Please ring 2C when you arrive and ill buzz you in.

- Please let me know your expected check in time as soon as possible (at least 2 days prior to arrival) and again 1 hour before arrival (or when you  are at the airport).

- If your desired check in time is earlier than 3 pm, please confirm that your check in time will work with me as soon as you can.

- Please also let me know your expected check out time as soon as you can.

- Please try to be discreet when you come in and out of the building and act normal, like you live here. 

- Please let me know, if you have recently had any COVID-19 symptoms or been in contact with anyone who has.  

- Please follow all federal and state COVID regulations.

- Please review the House Manual for WiFi, House Rules, and other information such as how to use the keys.  Please make sure to share this information with any other guests. HOUSE MANUAL:

Your room is the first room (first door) when you go down the hallway.

Wifi: GodBlessAmerica
Pw: courage22

Keys:  gold key is for the two main entrance doors; silver key is for the 2C apt door (bottom lock).  For all doors, turn the keys counterclockwise and push (no need to turn the door knob).  Please lock bottom lock when leaving the apartment.  **Please do not lock or touch the top lock.**

COVID precautions:  please wash or sanitize your hands when you enter the apartment.  Sanitizer and alcohol spray are available on the kitchen table. 

Quiet Hours:  between 12 am and 9 am (just a general rule, not strictly enforced).

Shower: Please close the shower curtain fully to avoid getting the floor wet. 

Bathroom (for  men):  please only use the toilet in the seated position (for sanitary reasons).

Windows: can be opened from top or bottom (if opening from top, push lock in the middle to open).

Electronics: please turn off all lights and electronics, especially the *electric heater*, before you leave the room.  Please also do not place anything in front of the electric heater to avoid fires.

Bedding/towels:  clean bed sheets will be provided.  You will be provided with one mattress cover, one duvet cover, and two pillow cases.  The white pillow covers that are already on the pillow are used for extra protection - please do not remove these and just place the pillow cases on top of these.  Before moving out, if you have time, please place all used bedsheets in the laundry basket.  Please DO NOT use any other clean bedsheets or towels in the closet.

Iron and steamer:  can be found in a box under the bathroom sink.  **Please use responsibly** (don’t leave them on and unattended, always turn off after use!), and place back exactly as you found them.  Ironing pad can be used on your desk.

CAUTION:  the metal pipes in the bathroom, kitchen and bedroom are part of the building’s central heating system and can get very hot.  Please do not touch them.

Shoes:  we wear shoes in the hallway, kitchen and bathroom (but you room floor will be clean).  Please ask for disposable slippers if you need them, for the hallway/kitchen/bathroom.

Check out:  please provide your expected check out time; please leave the keys on the desk when you check out.

Garbage disposal:  small trash you can leave in the room or kitchen.  Big trash, please take down to the trash room in the basement (take the elevators to “B”; please make sure to recycle properly to avoid issues with building management).

Laundry:  two options.  First, there is a laundry room in the basement that is open from 9 am to 9:30 pm (last wash at around 7:30 pm).  You can use the laundry detergent under the sink.  Second, there is a laundromat on 965 Amsterdam avenue that’s open until midnight (last wash at around 10 pm).

Pharmacy/Supermarket:  if you need any toothbrushes, makeup remover, hair products, water, beer, etc there is a 24 hour Duane Reade pharmacy at the corner of W 111th St and Broadway.  There is also a 24 hr supermarket (West Side Market) on W 110th and Broadway. 

Please clean up after yourself in the common areas, and after using any dishes/cups/utensils.

Please try not to leave any heavy makeup stains on the towels or sheets - they are very hard to wash off.

Kitchen: you are welcome to use anything in the kitchen (fridge, water, microwave, plates, pots, utensils, etc).  
You are welcome to do light cooking, as long as you clean up fully after yourself.

Please don’t hesitate to contact me if you have any questions.

Make yourself at home and enjoy your stay!  Hey

qa_pairs.a:
Hi again [GUEST] . Just wanted to give you some info on getting from the airport to Cascais. If you want to take public transport, you want to take the Subway to Cais Do sodre, and then take the train to Cascais (the last stop). It's about a 95 min journey including a 10 min walk in Cascais. Alternatively, Bolt and Uber are options. Bolt is considerably cheaper than Bolt with prices ranging from 25 to 35 euros depending on the time of the day. That's about a 35 min journey.

As an additional reference, the house is located directly in front of the restaurant Amazem 22. 

You can find additional info on getting to the house and around Sintra/Lisbon/Porto on our website:

https://www.casarosahouse.com/getting-to-from-sintra-lisbon-and-porto-without-a-car/

Happy travels. 

Ricado

qa_pairs.q:
Ridesharing is cheap in Portugal when relative to other Western European countries or the US. Uber and Bolt are the main ones, with Bolt 20% cheaper than Uber on average. A Bolt ride to Lisbon can be as low as 18€ and should be a bit cheaper to Sintra. You will need to take XL to fit 6 people which is going to be a bit more. We also started taking friends who don't have much time to Quinta da Regaleira instead of Palace of Pena (which is the most frequently visited) simply because it is less crowded and doesn't have the lines of tourists to get in. They are different, but both very impressive in their own way. This is amazing!!! Thank you very very much! My pleasure. Ask away if you have more questions please, not just about Cascais. Full name, date of birth, nationality, country of residence, passport number, passport issue date. Thanks for your understanding on this.

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] . Sorry, I couldn't be there to greet you. I hope you find everything well in the apartment and that you are loving Cascais.

Here are my top recommendations for restaurants, not in any particular order.

- Dom [GUEST] I restaurant, just a few meters towards the water on Beco Dos Invalidos (same little road as the main door to the house). Great seafood. I like their tuna. Only thing you might find strange depending on where you are from is that their fish comes with the head, but that's almost everywhere in Portugal.

- If you like Indian, Masala Indian on the main pedestrian street. I've tried every Indian restaurant in Cascais, and not only this has the best location, but the best food.

- Capricciosa pizza on the beach. They have 2 entrances. One from the top and one from the bottom. If you are lucky to go to the top and sit by the balcony, you have an amazing view. And pizza is good.

- Kul. This place has reinvented itself a few times over the past year. But last time we tried it. We had the octopus, pizza and seafood and it was good.  It's on the square. Amazing location. We've been disappointed with more than 1 place in that plaza. As you can imagine, when you own that location, you probably don't have to try too hard. But this place had great service and good food.

- Amori D'ana. Has good crepes, good crepes with ice cream and good ice cream.  Our son likes this place. And has very friendly service.

- For breakfast. We like Bijou. They open early and you can sit across from it where they have umbrellas. Good pastries.  

Please let us know if you have any questions!

qa_pairs.a:
Hi [GUEST] and Paulette,

About a breakfast place: Bijou de Cascais. They open at 8 AM and notice that they have tables in the square with umbrellas. It's much nicer than sitting inside or at the tables right outside:
https://g.page/BijouDeCascais?share

One lunch place I forgot to tell you is [GUEST] . Food is good, prices are reasonable for the location and the view to the beach is excellent. 

Another place with a great view is Capricciosa, an Italian Pizzeria. They have seating right at the beach and at the top (you have to enter from the top to sit on the top). It can get crowded though, but if you can get seating outside from the top it is a magnificent view and the Pizza is great:
https://goo.gl/maps/qoVd1SALdnjkw2Tq5

The websites I suggest for rentals:
https://www.imovirtual.com/arrendar/moradia/cascais-e-estoril-cascais/
I don't see an option for English on the first. But I do see a few medium term rentals in Cascais. They have listings all over Portugal.

and the most well known one is:
https://www.idealista.pt/
They have lots of rentals, but not sure if any are medium term rentals.

Also, another thing you could try, if you are thinking about Cascais for a medium term rental is connect with a realtor that works the area. I am not sure if they do short term rentals, but you could ask. MAP is probably the biggest agent in Cascais. Their office is a few meters from the house:
https://goo.gl/maps/SvhqCyfNVUfSFFtf6

Enjoy and please don't hesitate to ask questions,

qa_pairs.a:
Step one: find the house.

Remember the address is:

Plaza de la encarnacion

number 28 ....… .4 left.

Maybe the numbers get confusing in the plaza. The easiest way to find the house is if you use google map or similar and search for "Restaurante Yamazaky Sevilla"

It's in the same building, the house is red and white.

You can see an image here:

https://www.dropbox.com/s/rsf74oxvoqzw6a2/20160804_000311.jpg?dl=0

Step Two: Open the first door.

To open the wooden door the code is 1218A.

Step three: take your keys

If you arrive on time, ring the bell 4 Left and we'll open the door. But if you arrive when I'm not home, I'll leave your keys in the safe box inside the building, the code is 7348 (PLEASE PUT BACK TO 0000 the numbers after you get the keys).

Please take the keys and go to the 4th floor. (The elevator is on the first floor)

The house door is outside the building to your left.

Sometimes, the glass and iron door is a bit tricky to open, you just have to try to place the key a little more than halfway and turn it gently.

Check:

Checkout time is at 12:00 (leave the keys at home or in the same box going down the stairs)

It's difficult for us to store your luggage for longer after your checkout. I hope you're understanding about this.

To leave your luggage for longer there's a place near the house and it's cheap. I'll leave you the address in case you need to use it.

If you have any problem or need anything, please write to me here.

Thanks.
